description: SQL Injection in login parameter allows attacker to extract or modify sensitive database records leading to full data breach
Reflected Cross-Site Scripting enables an attacker to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by victims stealing session cookies
Stored XSS allows persistent malicious script injection that executes in victim browsers compromising user accounts at scale
Directory traversal exposes sensitive files and configuration data enabling attackers to read arbitrary server files
Blind SQL Injection via time-based technique allows unauthorized extraction of database contents without visible output
CRLF Injection in HTTP headers allows response splitting and log injection attacks enabling phishing and cache poisoning
Server-Side Request Forgery allows attacker to force server to make internal network requests exposing internal services
Cross-Site Request Forgery tricks authenticated users into performing unintended actions changing account settings or transactions
Missing Content Security Policy header allows malicious script injection through XSS expanding attack surface
Weak credentials allow brute force attacks enabling unauthorized access to application accounts and admin panels
Insecure HTTP headers expose server version and allow clickjacking attacks reducing security posture significantly
Missing SSL/TLS allows man-in-the-middle attacks exposing sensitive data transmitted in plaintext over the network
Weak cipher suites allow cryptographic downgrade attacks enabling decryption of intercepted encrypted communications
Exposed backup files reveal source code and database credentials allowing attackers to gain full system access
Command injection allows execution of arbitrary OS commands on the server enabling complete system compromise
XXE injection exploits XML parser to read sensitive server files or perform SSRF attacks on internal network
Open redirection allows attackers to redirect users to malicious websites facilitating phishing credential theft
Missing HttpOnly cookie flag exposes session cookies to client-side script access enabling session hijacking via XSS
Outdated server software contains known vulnerabilities that can be exploited for unauthorized access or denial of service
Path traversal via file parameter allows local file inclusion reading sensitive server configuration and credential files
Unprotected admin directory accessible without authentication allows complete administrative control of application
Missing X-Frame-Options header enables clickjacking attacks tricking users into unintended interactions with the application
Information disclosure through verbose error messages reveals internal stack traces database schema and server paths
Broken authentication allows session fixation or credential stuffing attacks leading to unauthorized account access
